title: SSC CGL T-I GA Section Test

questions.question: India’s participation at Davos 2026 is primarily aimed at which of the following obwectives?
options: Attracting investment and showcasing growth models
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) Attracting investment and showcasing growth models Explanation: • India uses Davos to attract foreign investment and highlight its economic reforms. • Several state governments and corporate leaders represent India. • States showcase opportunities in infrastructure, energy, and industry. Information Booster: • Indian companies like Tata, Infosys, Mahindra, and JSW participate actively. • States such as Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Maharashtra promote investments. Additional Knowledge: • Davos is a key platform for economic diplomacy. • India positions itself as a reliable global growth partner.

questions.question: The portable water purification system ‘SWaDeS’, recently developed for Indian soldiers, is significant mainly because of which of the following features?
options: It operates only on solar energy and is meant for civilian use
It replaces large-scale desalination plants used by the Navy
It is designed exclusively for disaster relief operations
It can desalinate seawater and purify CBRN-contaminated water in remote areas
questions.explanation: Correct Answer (d) It can desalinate seawater and purify CBRN-contaminated water in remote areas Explanation • The Sea Water Desalination System (SWaDeS) has been developed by Defence Laboratory Jodhpur under Defence Research and Development Organisation. • It is a portable water purification system designed to supply safe drinking water to soldiers deployed in remote, coastal, and water-scarce regions. • A key strength of SWaDeS is its CBRN (Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear) water purification module, which ensures water safety even in contaminated or high-risk environments. Information Booster • SWaDeS is available in two variants: • Manual-operated: supports 10–12 personnel, suitable for power-scarce areas • Engine-operated: supports 20–25 personnel, deployable within 2–3 minutes • The engine-operated variant can reduce TDS levels from ~35,000 mg/L (seawater) to below 500 mg/L, making water potable. • Both variants have successfully completed Acceptance Test Procedures (ATP), confirming field reliability. Additional Knowledge • SWaDeS is suitable for naval operations, coastal installations, long-range patrols, and inland saline lakes such as Pangong Tso. • Portable desalination systems are crucial for logistics independence of armed forces, especially in forward and isolated locations. • The development strengthens self-reliance in defence technology and aligns with India’s focus on indigenous solutions for operational readiness.

questions.question: Consider the following: 1. Gwalior Gharana is the oldest Khayal gharana. 2. Patiala Gharana is known for intricate taans and bol-bant. 3. Agra Gharana focuses exclusively on instrumental music.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (a) 1 and 2 only. • Gwalior Gharana is the oldest and most influential Khayal Gharana in Hindustani classical music. • Patiala Gharana is known for its intricate taans, bol-bant, and ornamentation in singing. • Agra Gharana does not focus exclusively on instrumental music; it is a vocal gharana. Information Booster: • Gharanas are schools or styles of Indian classical music. • Gwalior Gharana was founded by Nathan Pir Baksh and Hassu Khan. • Patiala Gharana was founded by Miyan Kallu and popularized by Bade Ghulam Ali Khan. • Agra Gharana evolved from Dhrupad tradition and emphasizes nom-tom alap. • Main Khayal Gharanas: Gwalior, Agra, Kirana, Patiala, Jaipur-Atrauli, and Rampur-Sahaswan. Additional Knowledge: • “Khayal” means imagination in Arabic/Persian. • Gwalior Gharana emphasizes clarity of notes and steady tempo. • Patiala Gharana features decorative murkis and fast rhythmic patterns. • Kirana Gharana focuses on melody and slow elaboration. • Agra Gharana integrates Dhrupad elements with Khayal singing.

questions.question: Consider an obwect moving along a straight line, it starts from point A, reaches point B, and then returns to point A. If the distance between A and B is 20 m , then the net displacement of the obwect is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (B) 0 m Explanation: Displacement is a vector quantity, which means it takes both magnitude and direction into account. If an obwect moves from point A to point B and then returns to point A, the total displacement is the straight-line distance between the starting and ending points in a specific direction. Since the obwect returns to the same position (point A), the net displacement is zero. Information Booster: • Displacement refers to the shortest straight-line distance between the initial and final positions, along with the direction. • The total distance traveled by the obwect is the sum of the distances between A and B and back, but displacement does not consider the total path length. • The formula for displacement in one-dimensional motion is: Displacement = Final position - Initial position. • Displacement can be negative, positive, or zero, depending on the direction of motion relative to the reference point.​

questions.question: MahaCrimeOS AI, recently launched by the Maharashtra Government in partnership with Microsoft, is primarily designed for which purpose?
options: Predicting natural disasters in coastal regions
Automating GST compliance in the state
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (b) Managing and accelerating cybercrime investigations. • The article states that MahaCrimeOS AI is an Azure-powered platform to modernise cybercrime case handling. • It streamlines digital investigation workflows. • Maharashtra aims to standardise and speed up all cybercrime investigations through this system. Information Booster: • First deployed in 23 police stations in Nagpur. • Achieved 80% reduction in investigation time. • Enables near 100% digital registration of cases.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution can the Parliament legislate on State List matters in national interest, if the Council of States passes a resolution by a two-thirds mawority?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) Article 249 Explanation: ● Under Article 249, the Rawya Sabha (Council of States) can empower the Parliament to make laws with respect to a matter in the State List in the national interest. ● This requires a resolution supported by not less than two-thirds of the members present and voting. ● Such a law remains in force for a period not exceeding one year, though it can be renewed. Information Booster: ● This is one of the exclusive powers of the Rawya Sabha. ● Article 356: Relates to the imposition of President's Rule in a state. Additional Knowledge: ● Normally, the State Legislature has exclusive power to make laws on subwects in the State List (Article 246).

questions.question: How does the Faint Young Sun paradox challenge our understanding of early Earth’s climate?
options: It implies there was no liquid water on early Earth.
It proves the Moon once had an atmosphere.
It suggests that early solar output was too weak to prevent global freezing.
It shows Earth was too hot due to excessive solar radiation.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (c) It suggests that early solar output was too weak to prevent global freezing Explanation: · The Faint Young Sun Paradox arises because about 4 billion years ago, the Sun’s luminosity was only ~70% of today’s. · This weaker Sun should have left Earth frozen solid, yet geological evidence shows liquid water and a relatively warm climate existed. · This paradox challenges climate models and suggests additional warming mechanisms (like greenhouse gases) were at play. Information Booster: · Term introduced by astronomer Carl Sagan and George Mullen (1972). · Proposed solutions include higher concentrations of CO₂, CH₄, NH₃ in the early atmosphere. · Geological evidence: ancient sedimentary rocks show presence of liquid water. · Volcanic outgassing and reduced continental landmass may have influenced warming. · The paradox is key in studies of early Earth habitability and astrobiology.

questions.question: India recently assumed the Chairmanship of which organisation from Bangladesh during the 13th Governing Council Meeting held in Malé, Maldives?
options: South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC)
Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (D) Bay of Bengal Inter-Governmental Organisation (BOBP-IGO) Explanation: • India formally took over the Chairmanship of the BOBP-IGO from Bangladesh during the 13th Governing Council Meeting held in Malé, Maldives, in February 2025. • The Indian delegation was led by Dr. Abhilaksh Likhi, Secretary of the Department of Fisheries, who reaffirmed India's commitment to sustainable fisheries management. • The meeting was part of a high-level conference focused on "Mainstreaming Ecosystem Approach to Fisheries Management (EAFM)" in small-scale fisheries. Information Booster: • Establishment: BOBP-IGO was set up in 2003 (evolving from an FAO programme of 1979) and is headquartered in Chennai, India. • Mandate: It serves as a regional fisheries advisory body to assist member countries (India, Bangladesh, Maldives, and Sri Lanka) in improving the livelihoods of small-scale fisherfolk. • Core Focus: Its primary goals include tackling Illegal, Unreported, and Unregulated (IUU) fishing and promoting the Blue Economy through regional cooperation. • Viksit Bharat 2047: This leadership role is seen as a step toward India’s goal of becoming a developed nation by 2047 through maritime and economic leadership. Additional Knowledge: • (A) SAARC: An 8-member regional intergovernmental organization in South Asia; its activities have been largely stalled due to regional tensions. • (B) IORA: A 23-member international organization of countries bordering the Indian Ocean, focusing on maritime security and trade. • (C) BIMSTEC: A regional group of 7 nations in the Bay of Bengal region focusing on multi-sectoral technical and economic cooperation, including trade and connectivity.

questions.question: In November 2025, India launched Operation Sagar Bandhu to provide humanitarian aid to which country after severe flooding caused by Cyclone Ditwah?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (A) Sri Lanka Explanation: • Operation Sagar Bandhu was initiated by the Indian Navy and Ministry of External Affairs in November 2025. • The mission aimed to provide immediate relief to Sri Lanka following the devastation caused by Cyclone Ditwah. • India dispatched naval ships carrying food, medicines, and portable water purification plants. • This operation aligns with India's 'Neighborhood First' policy and its role as a first responder in the Indian Ocean Region. • Specialized medical teams from the Indian Army were also deployed to assist in flood-hit provinces. Information Booster: • India's SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision focuses on maritime cooperation and disaster response. • HADR (Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief) operations are a core component of Indian naval diplomacy. Additional Knowledge: • Afghanistan (Option B): India has provided wheat and medical aid via 'Operation Devi Shakti' and land routes, but not for cyclone relief. • Bhutan (Option C): India shares strong ties here but relief is usually land-based for seismic or landslide events. • Myanmar (Option D): India previously launched 'Operation Karuna' to help Myanmar after Cyclone Mocha in 2023.

questions.question: Match the following sportsmen/sportswomen with their respective sports: 1. Julia Görges – i. Golf 2. Brook Koepka – ii. Football 3. Eden Hazard – iii. Tennis
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (a) 1(iii), 2(i), 3(ii) Explanation: ● Julia Görges: A retired professional tennis player from Germany. ● Brooks Koepka: A world-renowned golf player from the USA. ● Eden Hazard: A famous football player from Belgium (formerly Real Madrid/Chelsea). Information Booster: ● Hazard was known as one of the best wingers in the world. ● Koepka has won multiple mawor championships in golf. Additional Knowledge: ● Tennis Grand Slams: Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, US Open.

questions.question: Consider the following about Indian rock-cut architecture: 1. The Awanta caves primarily contain Buddhist artworks. 2. The Ellora caves showcase Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain traditions. 3. The Barabar caves were made during the Mauryan period. 4. The Elephanta caves are famous for Hindu sculptures. Which statements are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (d) All of the above. 1. Correct – Awanta Caves (Maharashtra) contain Buddhist paintings and sculptures, mainly depicting Jataka tales. 2. Correct – Ellora Caves (Maharashtra) are unique as they house Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain monuments in one site. 3. Correct – Barabar Caves (Bihar), the oldest rock-cut caves in India, were built during the Mauryan period (Ashoka & Dasharatha). 4. Correct – Elephanta Caves (Maharashtra) are famous for Hindu sculptures, especially the Trimurti (three-faced Shiva). Information Booster: • Awanta → UNESCO site, famous for murals and frescoes (2nd century BCE–6th century CE). • Ellora → Largest cave complex (34 caves), built between 6th–10th centuries CE. • Barabar → Associated with Awivika sect, polished interiors. • Elephanta → 6th century CE, masterpiece of Shaivism art. • Rock-cut caves show India’s continuity of religious harmony in architecture. Additional Knowledge: • Awanta rediscovered by British officer John Smith in 1819. • Kailasa temple at Ellora (Cave 16) is the world’s largest monolithic structure. • Barabar caves show Mauryan polish similar to Ashokan pillars. • Elephanta caves declared UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1987.

questions.question: When was the cement industry in India totally decontrolled?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) 1989 Explanation: • The cement industry in India was totally decontrolled on 1 March 1989. • Prior to this, the government regulated price and distribution under the Cement Control Order, 1956. • In 1982, partial decontrol was introduced through a dual pricing system (levy and free market cement). • The 1989 reform removed all price and distribution controls, allowing market forces to determine supply and prices. • This led to increased production, reduced shortages, and growth of the cement sector. Information Booster: • The cement industry was delicensed in 1991 as part of the New Economic Policy reforms. • Decontrol helped India become one of the largest cement producers globally. Additional Knowledge: 1982 (Option a) • Marked partial decontrol of the cement industry. • Introduced dual pricing system. • Not complete decontrol. 1991 (Option b) • Year of industrial delicensing under LPG reforms. • Cement industry licensing requirement removed. • Decontrol had already happened earlier.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution: 1. Right to Equality is provided under Article 14-18. 2. Right to Freedom includes the freedom of speech and expression. 3. Right against exploitation prohibits human trafficking and child labor. 4. Right to Property is a Fundamental Right. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (a) 1, 2 and 3 only. 1. Correct – Articles 14–18 provide the Right to Equality. 2. Correct – Article 19(1)(a) provides freedom of speech and expression, part of the Right to Freedom (Articles 19–22). 3. Correct – Articles 23–24 under Right against Exploitation prohibit human trafficking, begar (forced labor), and child labor. 4. Incorrect – Right to Property was a Fundamental Right under Article 31 but was abolished by the 44th Constitutional Amendment (1978). It is now a legal right under Article 300A. Information Booster: • Six Fundamental Rights: Equality, Freedom, Against Exploitation, Freedom of Religion, Cultural & Educational Rights, Constitutional Remedies. • Fundamental Rights are wusticiable (can be enforced in courts). • Article 32 → Right to Constitutional Remedies (Dr. Ambedkar called it the "heart & soul" of the Constitution). • Rights can be suspended during National Emergency (except Articles 20 & 21). • Right to Property was the 7th Fundamental Right before being removed. Additional Knowledge: • Article 14 → Equality before law & equal protection of laws. • Article 19 → Six freedoms (speech, assembly, association, movement, residence, profession). • Article 23 → Prohibition of trafficking & forced labor. • Article 24 → Prohibition of child labor in factories & mines.

questions.question: Which of the following are the key factors that are favourable for India for making it a key destination for foreign investments? 1. India’s robust economic fundamentals 2. Ongoing structural reforms 3. Decreasing consumer market position
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: (B) 1 and 2 only Explanation: ● India attracts foreign investment due to strong economic fundamentals and ongoing structural reforms such as GST, IBC, and PLI schemes. ● India’s consumer market is expanding, not decreasing – hence statement 3 is not favourable for FDI. ● Therefore, only statements 1 and 2 support foreign investment. Information Booster: ● India is one of the top FDI destinations globally (as per UNCTAD). ● The PLI scheme aims to boost manufacturing and exports. ● Over 65% of India’s population is under 35 years, offering a huge workforce. ● Digital India & Make in India increased ease of doing business. ● India received over $70 billion FDI in FY 2024, mainly in services, IT & telecom.

questions.question: In December 2025, the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC) approved key bureaucratic appointments. Who among the following was appointed as Director General (DG) of the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT)?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer(c) Lav Aggarwal Explanation • The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet, chaired by Narendra Modi, appointed Lav Aggarwal as the Director General of DGFT under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry. • He succeeded Away Bhadoo, who was holding additional charge after the transfer of Santosh Kumar Sarangi. Information Booster • Lav Aggarwal is a 1996-batch IAS officer of the Andhra Pradesh cadre. • He served as Joint Secretary, Ministry of Health and Family Welfare from 2016 to 2022. • He earlier worked as Additional Secretary in the Ministry of Women and Child Development. Additional Knowledge • In the same reshuffle, Rabindra Kumar Agarwal was appointed Chairman and Managing Director (CMD) of Food Corporation of India, replacing Ashutosh Agnihotri. • Rabindra Kumar Agarwal is a 1997-batch IAS officer of the Kerala cadre and previously served as Additional Secretary, Ministry of Cooperation, and Central Registrar of Cooperative Societies (CRCS). • FCI, established in 1965 under the Food Corporation Act, 1964, functions under the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution with headquarters in New Delhi.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary obwective of the Jal Jeevan Mission?
options: To promote rainwater harvesting and water conservation in rural areas
To provide functional household tap connections to all rural households
To desalinate seawater and providing to every rural household
To ensure proper water supply and sanitation in villages
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (b) To provide functional household tap connections to all rural households Explanation: ● The Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) was launched by PM Narendra Modi on August 15, 2019. ● Its primary obwective is to provide Functional Household Tap Connections (FHTC) to every rural household by 2024. ● The mission ensures a supply of 55 litres of water per person per day. Information Booster: ● It is implemented by the Ministry of Jal Shakti. ● The program also focuses on source sustainability, such as greywater management and rainwater harvesting. Additional Knowledge: ● Goa was the first 'Har Ghar Jal' certified state in India.

questions.question: Read the statements about the Goncha Festival of Bastar: 1.) Tupki, a mock gun made of bamboo, uses Goncha fruit as bullets. 2.) The chariot procession was started by Chalukya rulers. 3.) Rath Yatra begins on Ashadh Shukla Dashmi and ends on Dutiya. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (a) Only 1 and 2. • Tupki uses Goncha fruit (wild berries) as bullets during celebrations, making statement 1 correct. • The Rath Yatra tradition in Bastar is believed to have started under Chalukya rulers, making statement 2 correct. • Rath Yatra begins on Ashadh Shukla Dwitiya, not Dashmi, so statement 3 is incorrect. Information Booster • Goncha Festival is celebrated in Jagdalpur, Bastar district, Chhattisgarh. • It coincides with the Jagannath Rath Yatra. • The festival reflects tribal martial traditions of Bastar. • Goncha fruit grows in local forest regions of Bastar. Additional Knowledge • Bastar region is known for Dussehra of Bastar, the longest Dussehra festival in India. • Chalukyas ruled parts of central India between 6th–12th century CE. • Rath Yatra is primarily associated with Lord Jagannath of Puri, Odisha. • Tribal festivals often integrate local weapons, forests, and folklore.

questions.question: In which sport did Deepika Kumari earn her 12th individual World Cup medal at the 2025 Shanghai World Cup?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (a) Explanation • Deepika Kumari is a celebrated Indian athlete associated with Archery. • She has represented India in multiple Olympics and won numerous medals in World Cups and Commonwealth Games. Information Booster • She was a former World No. 1 archer. • Archery involves shooting arrows with a bow at a target; precision and focus are critical. Additional Knowledge • Table Tennis (b): Famous Indian players include Manika Batra and Sharath Kamal. • Badminton (c): Famous Indian players include P.V. Sindhu and Saina Nehwal. • Shooting (d): Famous Indian players include Abhinav Bindra and Manu Bhaker.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment introduced the Tenth Schedule to the Indian Constitution, commonly known as the Anti-Defection Law, to curb political defections by legislators?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) 52nd Amendment Act, 1985 Explanation: ● The 52nd Amendment Act added the Tenth Schedule, which provides for the disqualification of members of Parliament and State Legislatures on the ground of defection from one political party to another. Information Booster: ● The 91st Amendment Act (2003) strengthened this law by removing the split provision (where 1/3rd of members could split without disqualification). ● Decisions on disqualification are made by the Speaker or the Chairman of the House. Additional Knowledge: ● 61st Amendment: Lowered voting age from 21 to 18. ● 73rd Amendment: Constitutional status to Panchayati Raw.

questions.question: Match the following: Instrument Type A. Mridangam 1. Percussion B. Shehnai 2. Wind C. Sarangi 3. String D. Jal Tarang 4. Percussion
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (d) A-1, B-2, C-3, D-4. • Mridangam – A double-headed percussion instrument used in Carnatic music. • Shehnai – A wind instrument commonly played at auspicious occasions. • Sarangi – A bowed string instrument from North India. • Jal Tarang – A melodic percussion instrument using water-filled bowls. Information Booster: • Mridangam accompanies Carnatic vocal and dance performances. • Shehnai was popularized by Ustad Bismillah Khan. • Sarangi produces sound close to the human voice. • Jal Tarang literally means “water waves.” • Indian musical instruments are classified into four types – Tata (string), Sushira (wind), Avanaddha (percussion), and Ghana (solid). Additional Knowledge: • Tabla and Pakhawaw are also percussion instruments. • Veena and Sitar are string instruments. • Flute and Nadaswaram are wind instruments. • Indian classical music has two main traditions – Hindustani (North) and Carnatic (South). • Instruments play a vital role in raga and tala presentation.

questions.question: Consider the following pairs: 1. 'The Argumentative Indian' — Amartya Sen 2. 'India Divided' — Rawendra Prasad 3. 'Discovery of India' — Maulana Azad
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) 1 and 2 only. • “The Argumentative Indian” was written by Amartya Sen, focusing on India’s intellectual and cultural history. • “India Divided” was authored by Dr. Rawendra Prasad, presenting his opposition to the partition of India. • “The Discovery of India” was written by Jawaharlal Nehru, not Maulana Azad. Information Booster: • The Argumentative Indian (2005) explores India’s history of debate, pluralism, and reason. • India Divided (1946) outlines the political, social, and moral obwections to India’s partition. • The Discovery of India (1944) was written by Nehru during his imprisonment in Ahmednagar Fort. • Maulana Abul Kalam Azad wrote India Wins Freedom. • These works are significant for understanding India’s intellectual and nationalist evolution. Additional Knowledge: • Amartya Sen received the Nobel Prize in Economics (1998). • Rawendra Prasad was India’s first President (1950–1962). • Jawaharlal Nehru’s Discovery of India inspired the TV series “Bharat Ek Khow.” • Maulana Azad served as India’s first Education Minister. • These literary works reflect mawor phases of India’s political and cultural awakening.

questions.question: On January 4, 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi virtually inaugurated the 72nd National Volleyball Tournament (Senior National Championship). Which of the following is a correct feature of this tournament?
options: It was hosted at Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium, New Delhi
It featured participation from 28 teams only
It was organised by the Volleyball Federation of India in collaboration with the Uttar Pradesh government
It was conducted exclusively for wunior-level players
questions.explanation: Correct Answer(c) It was organised by the Volleyball Federation of India in collaboration with the Uttar Pradesh government Explanation • The 72nd Senior National Volleyball Championship was inaugurated virtually by the Prime Minister from Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, at Dr. Sampurnanand Sports Stadium. • The tournament is wointly organised by the Volleyball Federation of India (VFI) and the Uttar Pradesh government, highlighting centre–state collaboration in sports promotion. Information Booster • The championship is being held from January 4 to January 11, 2026. • It features 58 teams representing states and institutions, with participation of over 1,000 players from across India. • Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath was present at the inaugural ceremony. Additional Knowledge • Two official mascots were unveiled: • ‘Nandu’ – inspired by the Nandi bull, symbolising strength and endurance • ‘Neera’ – inspired by the Ganga River Dolphin, symbolising environmental awareness • The Senior National Championship is one of India’s premier domestic volleyball tournaments, playing a key role in talent identification for national teams.

questions.question: If you visit a garden you will find different plants and animals. All these living organisms interact with each other and their growth, reproduction and other activities are affected by the abiotic components. So, garden is an example of:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) Artificial ecosystem Explanation: ● An Artificial Ecosystem (or man-made ecosystem) is one that is created and maintained by humans. ● Since a garden requires human intervention (planting, weeding, watering), it is artificial. Information Booster: ● Natural Ecosystems: Forests, Lakes, Oceans, Deserts (exist without human help). ● Other Artificial Ecosystems: Crop fields (farms), Aquariums, Zoos. Additional Knowledge: ● Abiotic components – Non-living parts like soil, water, sunlight, and temperature. ● Biotic components – Living parts like plants, animals, and bacteria.

questions.question: Kanchenwunga has four ridges extending in which directions, and what are the names of the glaciers located between them?
options: North, East, South, West; Zemu, Talung, Yalung, Kanchenwunga glaciers
Northeast, Southeast, Southwest, Northwest; Gangotri, Pindari, Satopanth, Bhagirathi glaciers
North, South, East, West; Siachen, Nubra, Zanskar, Drang-Drung glaciers
Northeast, Southeast, Southwest, Northwest; Zemu, Talung, Yalung, Kanchenwunga glaciers
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (d) Northeast, Southeast, Southwest, Northwest; Zemu, Talung, Yalung, Kanchenwunga glaciers. Explanation Kanchenwunga is characterized by four mawor ridges extending towards the northeast, southeast, southwest, and northwest. Nestled between these ridges are four prominent glaciers: the Zemu Glacier (northeast), Talung Glacier (southeast), Yalung Glacier (southwest), and Kanchenwunga Glacier (northwest). These glaciers are vital sources of freshwater and contribute to the unique ecological system of the region. Their positions between the ridges help shape the mountain’s distinctive topography and influence local climate patterns. Information Booster · Four ridges of Kanchenwunga extend northeast, southeast, southwest, and northwest. · The glaciers between them are Zemu, Talung, Yalung, and Kanchenwunga glaciers. · These glaciers contribute to the Himalayan river systems. · The Zemu Glacier is one of the largest glaciers in the eastern Himalayas. · Glaciers play a key role in sustaining biodiversity and local livelihoods. Additional Knowledge (a) North, East, South, West; Zemu, Talung, Yalung, Kanchenwunga glaciers: Incorrect because the ridges extend to the intermediate directions, not the cardinal directions. (b) Northeast, Southeast, Southwest, Northwest; Gangotri, Pindari, Satopanth, Bhagirathi glaciers: These glaciers are located in the western Himalayas, unrelated to Kanchenwunga. (c) North, South, East, West; Siachen, Nubra, Zanskar, Drang-Drung glaciers: These glaciers are in the western Himalayan region, not around Kanchenwunga. (d) Northeast, Southeast, Southwest, Northwest; Zemu, Talung, Yalung, Kanchenwunga glaciers: Correct description of Kanchenwunga’s ridges and surrounding glaciers.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. Auxins promote elongation of cells in shoots. 2. Gibberellins stimulate seed germination. 3. Abscisic acid promotes stomatal opening during drought stress. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) 1 and 2 only. Explanation: • Auxins (e.g., IAA) promote cell elongation—phototropism & apical dominance. • Gibberellins break seed dormancy and promote germination & stem elongation. • Abscisic acid (ABA) causes stomatal closure during drought (not opening). • Hence 1 & 2 true; 3 false. Information Booster: • Auxin produced in shoot tips; transported basipetally. • GA causes bolting in rosette plants & stimulates α-amylase in seeds. • ABA = “ stress hormone”; induces dormancy & anti-transpiration. • Other hormones: cytokinins (cell division), ethylene (fruit ripening). • Guard cells regulate stomata via turgor changes (K⁺ flux).
